examples.prompt: Search quality degraded 21 days ago. Since then, 50% of searches return zero results and 18% of users churn immediately after searching.
examples.useCase: Model product quality issues and their impact on user behavior

examples.prompt: Users in variant A for the video recommendation experiment watch 70% longer and are 75% more likely to subscribe compared to users in variant B
examples.useCase: Create realistic A/B test results with clear winner and loser variants

examples.prompt: After the improvement 15 days ago, checkout amounts increased 50% and video watch times increased 50%, but before that date, overall volume was down 33%
examples.useCase: Model product improvements and their impact on key metrics

examples.prompt: Users with custom theme have 3x more checkout events compared to users with light or dark themes
examples.useCase: Create correlations between user properties and behavior

examples.prompt: Users who watch low quality videos (480p, 360p, 240p) more than high quality videos have a 50% chance of churning halfway through their lifecycle
examples.useCase: Model churn based on product quality or engagement patterns

examples.prompt: AWS outage from October 19 to October 22 caused 88% of events to be dropped during that time window
examples.useCase: Simulate infrastructure failures and their data impact

examples.prompt: After the BYND stock squeeze on October 22, 10% of all trades are for that symbol
examples.useCase: Model new product launches and adoption curves

examples.prompt: Users who read marketing emails increase their purchase amounts by 10% per email read, up to a maximum of 50% total increase
examples.useCase: Model cumulative marketing campaign effects on user behavior

examples.prompt: Before the rate change 17 days ago, student loans were declined 60% of the time. After the change, student loans convert to auto loans 60% of the time, and auto loans get approved 60% of the time but take 1-20 minutes longer
examples.useCase: Engineer complex funnel behavior changes over time

examples.prompt: Users exposed to AI model Q use more products. Users exposed to model BERT use fewer products. Product count directly affects event volume: users with 3+ products have 40% more events, users with <3 products have 40% fewer events
examples.useCase: Model AI/ML feature impact on product adoption and engagement

examples.prompt: Users in the engineering department are admin 50% of the time, while users in sales have no access 50% of the time
examples.useCase: Create realistic role-based access patterns based on user attributes

examples.prompt: Users with 3 or more preferred features see NPS scores improve by 10% month over month
examples.useCase: Model progressive satisfaction improvements based on feature adoption

examples.prompt: Companies with 3 or more products experience 10% MRR growth every month. 20% of companies churn and their MRR drops to zero after a random month
examples.useCase: Model B2B revenue growth and churn patterns

examples.prompt: Accounts managed by CSM named AK have 50% higher MRR than other accounts
examples.useCase: Model CSM performance impact on account metrics

examples.prompt: Ensure experiment start events always appear first in funnel sequences to maintain proper attribution
examples.useCase: Maintain clean experiment attribution in funnel analysis

examples.prompt: Conversion rates were 50-90% of normal before the improvement 30 days ago. After the improvement, conversion rates progressively increased by 2% per day for 30 days, reaching up to 200% of baseline
examples.useCase: Model realistic product improvement curves in funnel performance

examples.prompt: Power users (who do more than 10 actions per week) have 2x higher event properties, 40% better funnel conversion rates, and never experience errors. Regular users have normal metrics.
examples.useCase: Demonstrate composing multiple hook types to create sophisticated user segmentation with cross-cutting concerns
